item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents reject the statement throughout. Agent A consistently argues that social harmony and public good matter only within rights-based limits. Agent B repeats a similar civil-liberties frame in Indonesian, emphasizing speech, religion, minority protection, and the danger of majority or state power. No visible stance flip; B briefly intensifies wording at turn 4 but returns to the same final position.
